    50 | def run_simulation_logic(
    51 |     raw_input: dict, 
    52 |     monte_carlo_enabled: bool = True, 
    53 |     attacker_skill: float = 1.0, 
    54 |     harden_node: str = None,
    55 |     _skip_extensions: bool = False
    56 | ) -> dict:
    57 |     """
    58 |     Core execution logic for the simulation.
    59 |     """
    60 |     start_time_perf = time.perf_counter()
    61 |     request_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    62 | 
    63 |     # 1. Validation & Parsing
    64 |     try:
    65 |         validate_input(raw_input)
    66 |         arch = parse_input(raw_input["architecture"])
    67 |     except Exception as e:
    68 |         return {
    69 |             **get_error_response(e),
    70 |             "request_id": request_id,
    71 |             "version": VERSION
    72 |         }
    73 | 
    74 |     # 2. Graph Construction
    75 |     try:
    76 |         G = build_graph(arch)
    77 |     except Exception as e:
    78 |         return {
    79 |             "status": "error",
    80 |             "message": f"Graph construction failed: {str(e)}",
    81 |             "request_id": request_id,
    82 |             "version": VERSION
    83 |         }
    84 | 
    85 |     # 2b. Attack Surface Analysis (runs on graph, independent of simulation)
    86 |     attack_surface = analyze_attack_surface(G)
    87 | 
    88 |     # 3. Path Finding & Primary Simulation
    89 |     sim_result = simulate_attack(G, arch)
    90 |     
    91 |     # Handle scenario where no path is found
    92 |     if sim_result.get("error") or not sim_result.get("attack_path"):
    93 |         resilience = {
    94 |             "is_resilient": True,
    95 |             "reason": sim_result.get("error") or "No viable attack path found to high-value targets.",
    96 |             "confidence": 94
    97 |         }
    98 |         
    99 |         # Minimal valid output even for resilient systems
   100 |         return _build_resilient_response(request_id, VERSION, arch, resilience, start_time_perf, attack_surface)
   101 | 
   102 |     # 4. Detailed Analysis (Stage 2-3)
   103 |     score_result = calculate_risk(G, sim_result)
   104 |     time_result  = estimate_breach_time(sim_result["attack_steps"])
   105 |     conf_result  = calculate_confidence(G, sim_result, arch)
   106 |     biz_result   = compute_business_impact(sim_result, score_result, time_result, conf_result, arch)
   107 |     
   108 |     primary_path = sim_result["attack_path"]
   109 |     exploit_prob = compute_path_probability(G, primary_path, attacker_skill)
   110 |     
   111 |     # Reuse all_paths from attack_engine (avoids duplicate nx.all_simple_paths call)
   112 |     all_paths = sim_result.get("all_paths", [primary_path])
   113 | 
   114 |     # Choke Points & Centrality
   115 |     choke_points_data = analyze_choke_points(G, all_paths)
   116 |     # Standardize field names for frontend (centrality_score -> centrality)
   117 |     choke_points = []
   118 |     for cp in choke_points_data:
   119 |         choke_points.append({
   120 |             "node": cp["node"],
   121 |             "centrality": cp["centrality_score"],
   122 |             "path_dependency_percent": cp["path_dependency_percent"],
   123 |             "risk_reduction_if_hardened": cp["risk_reduction_if_hardened"],
   124 |             "single_point_of_catastrophic_failure": cp["single_point_of_catastrophic_failure"]
   125 |         })
   126 | 
   127 |     # Monte Carlo
   128 |     if monte_carlo_enabled:
   129 |         config = get_config()
   130 |         iterations = config.get("simulation", {}).get("monte_carlo_iterations", 200)
   131 |         monte_carlo_results = run_monte_carlo(G, all_paths, iterations=iterations, skill_multiplier=attacker_skill)
   132 |     else:
   133 |         monte_carlo_results = {"iterations": 0, "breach_success_rate": 0.0, "average_time_if_successful": "Disabled"}
   134 | 
   135 |     # Advanced Risk
   136 |     impact_mult = 1.0 + (len(biz_result.get("summary_tags", [])) * 0.1)
   137 |     advanced_risk = calculate_advanced_risk(
   138 |         structural_risk     = score_result["risk_score"],
   139 |         exploit_probability = exploit_prob,
   140 |         impact_multiplier   = impact_mult,
   141 |         time_to_breach      = time_result["total_minutes"]
   142 |     )
   143 | 
   144 |     # 5. Stage 4.5 Extensions
   145 |     timeline = generate_attack_timeline(time_result["breakdown"])
   146 |     path_clusters = cluster_attack_paths(all_paths, G)
   147 |     
   148 |     risk_components = {
   149 |         "structural_exposure": score_result["component_scores"].get("exposure_weight", 0),
   150 |         "intrinsic_vulnerability": score_result["component_scores"].get("asset_value_weight", 0),
   151 |         "target_proximity": score_result["component_scores"].get("depth_weight", 0),
   152 |         "privilege_amplification": score_result["component_scores"].get("privilege_weight", 0)
   153 |     }
   154 | 
   155 |     # Baseline for extensions
   156 |     baseline_metrics = {
   157 |         "risk_score": score_result["risk_score"],
   158 |         "paths": all_paths,
   159 |         "exploit_probability": exploit_prob
   160 |     }
   161 | 
   162 |     # Node Hardening Sandbox
   163 |     harden_metrics = None
   164 |     if harden_node and not _skip_extensions:
   165 |         harden_metrics = simulate_node_hardening(
   166 |             arch=arch, 
   167 |             node_id=harden_node, 
   168 |             baseline_metrics=baseline_metrics, 
   169 |             run_sim_func=lambda *a, **kw: run_simulation_logic(*a, **kw, _skip_extensions=True),
   170 |             attacker_skill=attacker_skill
   171 |         )
   172 | 
   173 |     # Sensitivity Analysis — lightweight scoring-only pass
   174 |     # Reuses existing graph and paths instead of re-running full simulation
   175 |     if _skip_extensions:
   176 |         sensitivity_results = {
   177 |             "analysis_type": "attacker_skill_sensitivity",
   178 |             "scenarios": [],
   179 |             "risk_variance": 0
   180 |         }
   181 |     else:
   182 |         sensitivity_results = _fast_sensitivity_analysis(
   183 |             G, sim_result, all_paths, arch, attacker_skill
   184 |         )
   185 | 
   186 |     # 6. AI Explanation
   187 |     ai_result = explain_attack(
   188 |         attack_path         = sim_result["attack_path"],
   189 |         secondary_path      = sim_result.get("secondary_attack_path", []),
   190 |         vulnerability_chain = sim_result["vulnerability_chain"],
   191 |         risk_score          = score_result["risk_score"],
   192 |         severity            = score_result["severity"],
   193 |         confidence_score    = conf_result["confidence_score"],
   194 |         breach_time         = time_result["display"],
   195 |         arch                = arch,
   196 |         business_impact     = biz_result,
   197 |     )
   198 | 
   199 |     # 7. Stage 4: Historical Validation & Defender Prioritization
   200 |     val_score, val_incident = HistoricalValidator().validate_path(sim_result["attack_path"])
   201 |     defender_prioritizer = DefenderPrioritization(G, all_paths)
   202 |     recommendations = defender_prioritizer.get_recommendations(top_n=5)
   203 | 
   204 |     # 7.5 RL Attacker (Stage 6)
   205 |     rl_attacker_data = {
   206 |         "rl_attack_path": [],
   207 |         "expected_reward": 0.0,
   208 |         "rl_vs_deterministic": "RL simulation disabled by config."
   209 |     }
   210 |     
   211 |     config = get_config()
   212 |     enable_rl = config.get("simulation", {}).get("enable_rl_agent", False)
   213 |     rl_episodes = config.get("simulation", {}).get("rl_episodes", 300)
   214 |     
   215 |     if enable_rl:
   216 |         try:
   217 |             # Use existing entry point and target for RL training
   218 |             rl_agent = train_rl_agent(G, [sim_result["entry_point"]], sim_result["target"], episodes=rl_episodes)
   219 |             rl_res = simulate_rl_path(rl_agent, sim_result["entry_point"], sim_result["target"])
   220 |             rl_attacker_data = {
   221 |                 "rl_attack_path": rl_res["path"],
   222 |                 "expected_reward": rl_res["reward"],
   223 |                 "rl_vs_deterministic": compare_rl_vs_deterministic(rl_res["path"], sim_result["attack_path"])
   224 |             }
   225 |         except Exception as e:
   226 |             rl_attacker_data["rl_vs_deterministic"] = f"RL simulation failed: {str(e)}"
   227 | 
   228 |     # 8. Final Output Assembly
   229 |     return {
   230 |         "status": "success",
   231 |         "request_id": request_id,
   232 |         "execution_time": round(time.perf_counter() - start_time_perf, 4),
   233 |         "version": VERSION,
   234 | 
   235 |         # Stage 6 RL Results
   236 |         "rl_attacker": rl_attacker_data,
   237 |         "rl_attack_path": rl_attacker_data["rl_attack_path"], # Flat for easy dashboard access
   238 |         "rl_expected_reward": rl_attacker_data["expected_reward"],
   239 |         "rl_comparison": rl_attacker_data["rl_vs_deterministic"],
   240 | 
   241 |         # Stage 4 Analytics
   242 |         "validation_score": val_score,
   243 |         "matched_incident": val_incident,
   244 |         "defender_recommendations": recommendations,
   245 | 
   246 |         # Paths
   247 |         "paths": all_paths, 
   248 |         "primary_path": sim_result["attack_path"],
   249 |         "secondary_paths": [sim_result.get("secondary_attack_path", [])] if sim_result.get("secondary_attack_path") else [],
   250 |         "attack_path": sim_result["attack_path"], # Legacy
   251 |         "attack_steps": sim_result["attack_steps"],
   252 |         "vulnerability_chain": sim_result["vulnerability_chain"],
   253 |         "entry_point": sim_result["entry_point"],
   254 |         "target": sim_result["target"],
   255 |         "attack_timeline": timeline,
   256 |         "path_clusters": path_clusters,
   257 |         "optimized_paths": sim_result.get("optimized_paths", {}),
   258 | 
   259 |         # Risk
   260 |         "risk_score": score_result["risk_score"],
   261 |         "structural_risk": score_result["risk_score"],
   262 |         "advanced_risk": advanced_risk,
   263 |         "severity": score_result["severity"],
   264 |         "severity_color": severity_color(score_result["severity"]),
   265 |         "component_scores": score_result["component_scores"],
   266 |         "risk_components": risk_components,
   267 | 
   268 |         # Analytics
   269 |         "exploit_probability": exploit_prob,
   270 |         "vulnerability_intelligence": collect_vulnerability_intelligence(G, primary_path),
   271 |         "breach_probability_overall": compute_overall_breach_probability(G, all_paths, attacker_skill),
   272 |         "monte_carlo_results": monte_carlo_results,
   273 |         "centrality_scores": {cp["node"]: cp["centrality"] for cp in choke_points},
   274 |         "choke_points": choke_points,
   275 |         "harden_metrics": harden_metrics,
   276 |         "sensitivity_analysis": sensitivity_results,
   277 |         "resilience_summary": {"is_resilient": False, "reason": "Attack path identified.", "confidence": conf_result["confidence_score"]},
   278 | 
   279 |         # Confidence
   280 |         "confidence_score": conf_result["confidence_score"],
   281 |         "confidence_label": conf_result["confidence_label"],
   282 |         "confidence_color": conf_result["confidence_color"],
   283 |         "confidence_factors": conf_result["factors"],
   284 | 
   285 |         # Breach Time
   286 |         "breach_time": time_result["display"],
   287 |         "breach_time_data": { # Keep object for frontend
   288 |             "display": time_result["display"],
   289 |             "total_minutes": time_result["total_minutes"],
   290 |             "breakdown": time_result["breakdown"],
   291 |         },
   292 | 
   293 |         # Impact
   294 |         "business_impact": biz_result,
   295 | 
   296 |         # AI Analysis (Standardized Keys)
   297 |         "ai_analysis": {
   298 |             "executive_summary": ai_result.get("executive_summary", ""),
   299 |             "technical_analysis": ai_result.get("technical_analysis", ""),
   300 |             "risk_justification": ai_result.get("risk_justification", ""),
   301 |             "business_interpretation": ai_result.get("business_interpretation", ""),
   302 |             "mitigation_roadmap": ai_result.get("mitigation_roadmap", ""),
   303 |             "error": ai_result.get("error")
   304 |         },
   305 |         "ai": ai_result, 
   306 |         "ai_explanation": ai_result.get("executive_summary", ""), # Legacy
   307 | 
   308 |         # Infrastructure
   309 |         "graph": _serialize_graph(G),
   310 |         "arch": arch,
   311 | 
   312 |         # Attack Surface
   313 |         "attack_surface": attack_surface
   314 |     }
   315 | 
   316 | 
   317 | def _fast_sensitivity_analysis(
   318 |     G: nx.DiGraph,
   319 |     sim_result: dict,
   320 |     all_paths: list,
   321 |     arch: dict,
   322 |     current_skill: float
   323 | ) -> dict:
   324 |     """
   325 |     Lightweight sensitivity analysis — reuses the existing graph and paths.
   326 |     Only recomputes scoring at different attacker skill levels instead of
   327 |     re-running the entire simulation pipeline.
   328 |     """
   329 |     skills = [0.6, 1.0, 1.4]
   330 |     skill_labels = ["Low", "Medium", "Elite"]
   331 |     results = []
   332 |     primary_path = sim_result["attack_path"]
   333 | 
   334 |     for skill, label in zip(skills, skill_labels):
   335 |         try:
   336 |             prob = compute_path_probability(G, primary_path, skill)
   337 |             # Risk score is structural (skill-independent), scale it by
   338 |             # a simple multiplier derived from probability change
   339 |             base_risk = calculate_risk(G, sim_result)["risk_score"]
   340 |             prob_factor = prob / max(compute_path_probability(G, primary_path, 1.0), 0.01)
   341 |             adjusted_risk = round(base_risk * max(0.5, min(prob_factor, 1.5)), 2)
   342 | 
   343 |             results.append({
   344 |                 "skill_level": label,
   345 |                 "skill_value": skill,
   346 |                 "risk_score": adjusted_risk,
   347 |                 "breach_probability": round(prob, 4)
   348 |             })
   349 |         except Exception:
   350 |             continue
   351 | 
   352 |     return {
   353 |         "analysis_type": "attacker_skill_sensitivity",
   354 |         "scenarios": results,
   355 |         "risk_variance": round(results[-1]["risk_score"] - results[0]["risk_score"], 2) if len(results) >= 2 else 0
   356 |     }
   357 | 
   358 | def _build_resilient_response(request_id, version, arch, resilience, start_time, attack_surface=None):
   359 |     return {
   360 |         "status": "success",
   361 |         "request_id": request_id,
   362 |         "execution_time": round(time.perf_counter() - start_time, 4),
   363 |         "version": version,
   364 |         "validation_score": None,
   365 |         "matched_incident": None,
   366 |         "defender_recommendations": [],
   367 |         "resilience_summary": resilience,
   368 |         "risk_score": 0,
   369 |         "structural_risk": 0,
   370 |         "advanced_risk": 0,
   371 |         "severity": "Low",
   372 |         "severity_color": severity_color("Low"),
   373 |         "paths": [],
   374 |         "primary_path": [],
   375 |         "primary_attack_path": [],
   376 |         "attack_path": [],
   377 |         "secondary_paths": [],
   378 |         "secondary_attack_path": [],
   379 |         "attack_steps": [],
   380 |         "vulnerability_chain": [],
   381 |         "entry_point": None,
   382 |         "target": None,
   383 |         "risk_components": {
   384 |             "structural_exposure": 0,
   385 |             "intrinsic_vulnerability": 0,
   386 |             "target_proximity": 0,
   387 |             "privilege_amplification": 0
   388 |         },
   389 |         "exploit_probability": 0,
   390 |         "vulnerability_intelligence": [],
   391 |         "breach_probability_overall": 0,
   392 |         "monte_carlo_results": {
   393 |             "iterations": 0,
   394 |             "breach_success_rate": 0,
   395 |             "average_time_if_successful": "N/A"
   396 |         },
   397 |         "centrality_scores": {},
   398 |         "choke_points": [],
   399 |         "harden_metrics": None,
   400 |         "optimized_paths": {},
   401 |         "confidence_score": resilience.get("confidence", 0),
   402 |         "confidence_label": "High",
   403 |         "confidence_color": "#2ecc71",
   404 |         "confidence_factors": {},
   405 |         "breach_time": "N/A",
   406 |         "breach_time_data": {
   407 |             "display": "N/A",
   408 |             "total_minutes": 0,
   409 |             "breakdown": []
   410 |         },
   411 |         "business_impact": {
   412 |             "data_risk": "None identified.",
   413 |             "operational_risk": "None identified.",
   414 |             "compliance_risk": "None identified.",
   415 |             "summary_tags": ["secure-architecture"]
   416 |         },
   417 |         "attack_timeline": [],
   418 |         "path_clusters": [],
   419 |         "sensitivity_analysis": {
   420 |             "analysis_type": "attacker_skill_sensitivity",
   421 |             "scenarios": [],
   422 |             "risk_variance": 0
   423 |         },
   424 |         "ai_analysis": {
   425 |             "executive_summary": "No viable attack paths identified. System architecture demonstrates strong defensive posture.",
   426 |             "technical_analysis": "Simulation could not find a path from any entry point to high-value targets.",
   427 |             "risk_justification": "Structural isolation and lack of public exposure reduce risk to zero.",
   428 |             "business_interpretation": "Operational continuity is likely maintained.",
   429 |             "mitigation_roadmap": "Maintain current security configurations and regular audits."
   430 |         },
   431 |         "ai": {
   432 |             "executive_summary": "No viable attack paths identified.",
   433 |             "technical_analysis": "No path found.",
   434 |             "risk_justification": "Zero risk.",
   435 |             "business_interpretation": "No impact.",
   436 |             "mitigation_roadmap": "None needed.",
   437 |             "technical_reasoning": "No path found.",
   438 |             "mitigation_strategy": "None needed.",
   439 |             "business_impact": "No impact.",
   440 |             "error": None
   441 |         },
   442 |         "ai_explanation": "No viable attack paths identified.",
   443 |         "graph": {"nodes": [], "edges": []},
   444 |         "arch": arch,
   445 |         "attack_surface": attack_surface or {"public_nodes": [], "high_risk_ports": [], "top_exposed_nodes": []}
   446 |     }
   447 | 
   448 | def _serialize_graph(G):
   449 |     nodes = []
   450 |     for name, data in G.nodes(data=True):
   451 |         nodes.append({
   452 |             "id"            : name,
   453 |             "permission"    : data.get("permission",     "medium"),
   454 |             "asset_value"   : data.get("asset_value",    1),
   455 |             "public_facing" : data.get("public_facing",  False),
   456 |             "exposure_score": round(data.get("exposure_score", 0), 3),
   457 |             "open_ports"    : data.get("open_ports",     []),
   458 |         })
   459 |     edges = [{"source": s, "target": t} for s, t in G.edges()]
   460 |     return {"nodes": nodes, "edges": edges}
   461 | 
   462 | if __name__ == "__main__":
   463 |     try:
   464 |         input_data = sys.stdin.read()
   465 |         if not input_data:
   466 |             print(json.dumps({"error": "No input provided via stdin"}))
   467 |             sys.exit(1)
   468 |             
   469 |         payload = json.loads(input_data)
   470 |         
   471 |         result = run_simulation_logic(
   472 |             raw_input      = payload,
   473 |             monte_carlo_enabled = payload.get("monte_carlo", True),
   474 |             attacker_skill = payload.get("attacker_skill", 1.0),
   475 |             harden_node    = payload.get("harden_node")
   476 |         )
   477 |         print(json.dumps(result))
   478 |         
   479 |     except json.JSONDecodeError:
   480 |         print(json.dumps({"error": "Invalid JSON input"}))
   481 |     except Exception as e:
   482 |         print(json.dumps({
   483 |             "status": "error",
   484 |             "message": f"Fatal engine error: {str(e)}",
   485 |             "trace": traceback.format_exc()
   486 |         }))
   487 | 


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: backend/rl_attacker.py
LINES: 228
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | """
     2 | rl_attacker.py — RL-based attacker module for discovering alternative attack paths.
     3 | Uses Q-Learning to learn optimal exploit sequences based on rewards.
     4 | """
     5 | 
     6 | import random
     7 | import networkx as nx
     8 | from typing import List, Dict, Tuple, Optional
     9 | 
    10 | 
    11 | class RLAttacker:
    12 |     """
    13 |     RL Agent that uses Q-Learning to discover high-reward attack paths in a network graph.
    14 |     """
    15 |     def __init__(
    16 |         self, 
    17 |         G: nx.DiGraph, 
    18 |         alpha: float = 0.2, 
    19 |         gamma: float = 0.8, 
    20 |         epsilon: float = 0.2,
    21 |         episodes: int = 1000,
    22 |         max_steps: int = 15
    23 |     ):
    24 |         """
    25 |         Initialize the RL Agent.
    26 |         
    27 |         Args:
    28 |             G: The network graph (Environment).
    29 |             alpha: Learning rate.
    30 |             gamma: Discount factor.
    31 |             epsilon: Exploration rate for epsilon-greedy policy.
    32 |             episodes: Number of training episodes.
    33 |             max_steps: Maximum steps allowed per episode.
    34 |         """
    35 |         self.G = G
    36 |         self.alpha = alpha
    37 |         self.gamma = gamma
    38 |         self.epsilon = epsilon
    39 |         self.episodes = episodes
    40 |         self.max_steps = max_steps
    41 |         
    42 |         # Q-Table: {state_node_id: {neighbor_node_id: q_value}}
    43 |         self.q_table = {}
    44 | 
    45 |     def _get_q_value(self, state: str, action: str) -> float:
    46 |         """Retrieve Q-value for a state-action pair, initializing if necessary."""
    47 |         if state not in self.q_table:
    48 |             self.q_table[state] = {neighbor: 0.0 for neighbor in self.G.neighbors(state)}
    49 |         return self.q_table[state].get(action, 0.0)
    50 | 
    51 |     def _set_q_value(self, state: str, action: str, value: float):
    52 |         """Update Q-value for a state-action pair."""
    53 |         if state not in self.q_table:
    54 |             self.q_table[state] = {neighbor: 0.0 for neighbor in self.G.neighbors(state)}
    55 |         self.q_table[state][action] = value
    56 | 
    57 |     def _choose_action(self, state: str) -> Optional[str]:
    58 |         """Epsilon-greedy action selection."""
    59 |         neighbors = list(self.G.neighbors(state))
    60 |         if not neighbors:
    61 |             return None
    62 |         
    63 |         if random.random() < self.epsilon:
    64 |             return random.choice(neighbors)
    65 |         
    66 |         # Greedy choice: pick action with highest Q-value
    67 |         q_values = [self._get_q_value(state, n) for n in neighbors]
    68 |         max_q = max(q_values)
    69 |         
    70 |         # Tie-breaking
    71 |         best_actions = [neighbors[i] for i, q in enumerate(q_values) if q == max_q]
    72 |         return random.choice(best_actions)
    73 | 
    74 |     def _calculate_reward(self, u: str, v: str, target_node: str) -> float:
    75 |         """
    76 |         Compute reward for moving from node u to node v.
    77 |         
    78 |         Rewards are grounded in CVSS/Vulnerability metrics where available.
    79 |         """
    80 |         v_data = self.G.nodes[v]
    81 |         vuln_score = v_data.get("vulnerability_score", 0.5)
    82 |         
    83 |         # Base reward: successful exploit (+1.0), scaled by vulnerability confidence
    84 |         reward = 1.0 + (vuln_score * 0.1)
    85 |         
    86 |         u_data = self.G.nodes[u]
    87 |         
    88 |         # Privilege escalation check (+0.1)
    89 |         u_perm = u_data.get("permission", "low")
    90 |         v_perm = v_data.get("permission", "low")
    91 |         perm_map = {"low": 0, "medium": 1, "high": 2}
    92 |         if perm_map.get(v_perm, 0) > perm_map.get(u_perm, 0):
    93 |             reward += 0.1
    94 |             
    95 |         # Lateral movement bonus (+0.05)
    96 |         reward += 0.05
    97 |         
    98 |         # Target node bonus (+0.2)
    99 |         if v == target_node:
   100 |             reward += 0.2
   101 |             
   102 |         # Extra bonus for high-value intermediate assets
   103 |         v_asset = v_data.get("asset_value", 1)
   104 |         if v_asset >= 8:
   105 |             reward += 0.1
   106 |             
   107 |         return reward
   108 | 
   109 |     def train(self, entry_points: List[str], target_node: str):
   110 |         """Train the RL agent using Q-Learning."""
   111 |         if not entry_points:
   112 |             return
   113 | 
   114 |         for _ in range(self.episodes):
   115 |             state = random.choice(entry_points)
   116 |             for _ in range(self.max_steps):
   117 |                 action = self._choose_action(state)
   118 |                 if not action:
   119 |                     break
   120 |                 
   121 |                 reward = self._calculate_reward(state, action, target_node)
   122 |                 next_state = action
   123 |                 
   124 |                 # Q-Learning update: Q(s,a) = Q(s,a) + alpha * [R + gamma * max(Q(s',a')) - Q(s,a)]
   125 |                 next_neighbors = list(self.G.neighbors(next_state))
   126 |                 max_next_q = 0.0
   127 |                 if next_neighbors:
   128 |                     max_next_q = max([self._get_q_value(next_state, n) for n in next_neighbors])
   129 |                 
   130 |                 old_q = self._get_q_value(state, action)
   131 |                 new_q = old_q + self.alpha * (reward + self.gamma * max_next_q - old_q)
   132 |                 self._set_q_value(state, action, new_q)
   133 |                 
   134 |                 state = next_state
   135 |                 if state == target_node:
   136 |                     break
   137 | 
   138 |     def get_best_path(self, entry_point: str, target_node: str) -> Tuple[List[str], float]:
   139 |         """Extract the best discovered path from the trained Q-table."""
   140 |         path = [entry_point]
   141 |         state = entry_point
   142 |         total_reward = 0.0
   143 |         visited = {entry_point}
   144 |         
   145 |         for _ in range(self.max_steps):
   146 |             neighbors = list(self.G.neighbors(state))
   147 |             if not neighbors:
   148 |                 break
   149 |             
   150 |             # Select greedy action from Q-table, avoiding cycles
   151 |             q_values = [self._get_q_value(state, n) for n in neighbors]
   152 |             
   153 |             # Filter neighbors to prevent infinite loops in path extraction
   154 |             valid_indices = [i for i, n in enumerate(neighbors) if n not in visited]
   155 |             if not valid_indices:
   156 |                 break
   157 |                 
   158 |             filtered_q = [q_values[i] for i in valid_indices]
   159 |             max_q = max(filtered_q)
   160 |             
   161 |             best_actions = [neighbors[valid_indices[i]] for i, q in enumerate(filtered_q) if q == max_q]
   162 |             action = random.choice(best_actions)
   163 |             
   164 |             total_reward += self._calculate_reward(state, action, target_node)
   165 |             state = action
   166 |             path.append(state)
   167 |             visited.add(state)
   168 |             
   169 |             if state == target_node:
   170 |                 break
   171 |                 
   172 |         return path, total_reward
   173 | 
   174 | 
   175 | def train_rl_agent(G: nx.DiGraph, entry_points: List[str], target_node: str, episodes: int = 1000) -> RLAttacker:
   176 |     """Helper function to train and return an RL attacker."""
   177 |     agent = RLAttacker(G, episodes=episodes)
   178 |     agent.train(entry_points, target_node)
   179 |     return agent
   180 | 
   181 | 
   182 | def simulate_rl_path(agent: RLAttacker, entry_point: str, target_node: str) -> Dict:
   183 |     """Helper function to get best path and reward from a trained agent."""
   184 |     path, reward = agent.get_best_path(entry_point, target_node)
   185 |     return {
   186 |         "path": path,
   187 |         "reward": round(reward, 3)
   188 |     }
   189 | 
   190 | 
   191 | def compare_rl_vs_deterministic(rl_path: List[str], det_path: List[str]) -> str:
   192 |     """Generate a textual explanation comparing RL results with deterministic models."""
   193 |     if not rl_path:
   194 |         return "RL agent failed to identify a viable path."
   195 |     
   196 |     if rl_path == det_path:
   197 |         return "The RL agent converged on the same optimal path as the deterministic model, validating the CVSS-grounded probability logic."
   198 |     
   199 |     if len(rl_path) < len(det_path) and det_path:
   200 |         return f"The RL agent discovered a more efficient shortcut ({len(rl_path)} steps) compared to the deterministic path ({len(det_path)} steps)."
   201 |     
   202 |     return "The RL agent prioritized cumulative reward (privilege gains and high-value intermediate assets) over the highest single-node exploit probabilities."
   203 | 
   204 | 
   205 | if __name__ == "__main__":
   206 |     # Demo test script
   207 |     print("--- RL Attacker Demo ---")
   208 |     G = nx.DiGraph()
   209 |     G.add_node("entry", permission="low", asset_value=1, public_facing=True)
   210 |     G.add_node("mid1", permission="medium", asset_value=5, public_facing=False)
   211 |     G.add_node("mid2", permission="low", asset_value=2, public_facing=False)
   212 |     G.add_node("target", permission="high", asset_value=10, public_facing=False)
   213 |     
   214 |     G.add_edge("entry", "mid1")
   215 |     G.add_edge("entry", "mid2")
   216 |     G.add_edge("mid1", "target")
   217 |     G.add_edge("mid2", "target")
   218 |     
   219 |     agent = train_rl_agent(G, ["entry"], "target", episodes=500)
   220 |     result = simulate_rl_path(agent, "entry", "target")
   221 |     
   222 |     print(f"RL Discovered Path: {' -> '.join(result['path'])}")
   223 |     print(f"Cumulative Reward: {result['reward']}")
   224 |     
   225 |     det_path = ["entry", "mid1", "target"]
   226 |     print(f"Deterministic Path: {' -> '.join(det_path)}")
   227 |     print(f"Comparison: {compare_rl_vs_deterministic(result['path'], det_path)}")
   228 | 


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: backend/defender_prioritization.py
LINES: 93
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | import json
     2 | import os
     3 | import networkx as nx
     4 | 
     5 | class DefenderPrioritization:
     6 |     def __init__(self, G, all_paths):
     7 |         self.G = G
     8 |         self.all_paths = all_paths
     9 |         
    10 |     def get_recommendations(self, top_n=5):
    11 |         """
    12 |         Analyzes attack paths to rank nodes by defense criticality.
    13 |         Returns top N nodes to patch/harden first with reasons and priority scores.
    14 |         """
    15 |         if not self.all_paths:
    16 |             return []
    17 |             
    18 |         # 1. Path Centrality: How many attack paths pass through this node?
    19 |         node_frequency = {}
    20 |         for path in self.all_paths:
    21 |             for node in path:
    22 |                 node_frequency[node] = node_frequency.get(node, 0) + 1
    23 |         
    24 |         # 2. Risk Scoring for Prioritization
    25 |         # risk_score = f(frequency_in_paths, exposure_score, vulnerability_presence, proximity_to_target)
    26 |         node_rankings = []
    27 |         
    28 |         # Find the target node from paths
    29 |         target_node = self.all_paths[0][-1]
    30 |         
    31 |         for node in self.G.nodes():
    32 |             # Skip entry/target nodes if they are synthetic/fixed
    33 |             if node.lower() in ["internet", "internal_user"]:
    34 |                 continue
    35 |                 
    36 |             node_data = self.G.nodes[node]
    37 |             
    38 |             # Base components
    39 |             frequency = node_frequency.get(node, 0) / len(self.all_paths) if self.all_paths else 0
    40 |             exposure  = node_data.get("exposure_score", 0.5)
    41 |             asset_val = node_data.get("asset_value", 1.0)
    42 |             
    43 |             # Vulnerability component (CVSS logic from Stage 2)
    44 |             cves = node_data.get("cves", [])
    45 |             vulnerability_factor = 0.5 # Default heuristic
    46 |             if cves:
    47 |                 vulnerability_factor = 0.85 # Higher if CVEs exist
    48 |             
    49 |             # Proximity factor
    50 |             try:
    51 |                 shortest_path_to_target = nx.shortest_path_length(self.G, source=node, target=target_node)
    52 |                 # Closer to target = higher priority (exponential decay)
    53 |                 proximity_score = 1.0 / (shortest_path_to_target + 1)
    54 |             except:
    55 |                 proximity_score = 0.05
    56 |             
    57 |             # Weighted priority calculation
    58 |             # frequency: 40%, exposure: 20%, vulnerability: 20%, proximity: 20%
    59 |             priority_score = (
    60 |                 (frequency * 0.4) + 
    61 |                 (exposure * 0.2) + 
    62 |                 (vulnerability_factor * 0.2) + 
    63 |                 (proximity_score * 0.2)
    64 |             ) * (asset_val / 5.0) # Scale by asset value
    65 |             
    66 |             # Normalized score (0-1)
    67 |             priority_score = min(priority_score, 1.0)
    68 |             
    69 |             # Generate reasoned explanation
    70 |             reasons = []
    71 |             if frequency > 0.5:
    72 |                 reasons.append(f"Crucial choke point in {int(frequency*100)}% of attack paths.")
    73 |             if cves:
    74 |                 reasons.append(f"Contains {len(cves)} known CVEs.")
    75 |             if proximity_score > 0.5:
    76 |                 reasons.append("High proximity to target asset.")
    77 |             if exposure > 0.7:
    78 |                 reasons.append("High public exposure score.")
    79 |                 
    80 |             if not reasons:
    81 |                 reasons = ["Intermediate node with significant lateral movement potential."]
    82 |                 
    83 |             node_rankings.append({
    84 |                 "node": node,
    85 |                 "reason": " ".join(reasons),
    86 |                 "priority_score": round(priority_score, 2)
    87 |             })
    88 |             
    89 |         # Sort by priority score descending
    90 |         sorted_rankings = sorted(node_rankings, key=lambda x: x["priority_score"], reverse=True)
    91 |         
    92 |         return sorted_rankings[:top_n]
    93 |

FILE: backend/historical_validation.py
LINES: 73
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | import json
     2 | import os
     3 | 
     4 | class HistoricalValidator:
     5 |     def __init__(self):
     6 |         # In-memory dataset of real-world breach patterns for Stage 4 validation.
     7 |         # Minimal set as requested: Log4Shell, SolarWinds, ProxyShell, PrintNightmare.
     8 |         self.breaches = [
     9 |             {
    10 |                 "incident_name": "Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228)",
    11 |                 "attack_chain": ["internet", "web_server", "app_server", "db_server"],
    12 |                 "description": "Unauthenticated RCE via LDAP/JNDI lookup leading to lateral movement and data exfiltration."
    13 |             },
    14 |             {
    15 |                 "incident_name": "SolarWinds Supply Chain (UNC2452)",
    16 |                 "attack_chain": ["internet", "update_server", "active_directory", "office365"],
    17 |                 "description": "Supply chain compromise via malicious update leading to identity provider access."
    18 |             },
    19 |             {
    20 |                 "incident_name": "ProxyShell (CVE-2021-34473)",
    21 |                 "attack_chain": ["internet", "exchange_server", "active_directory", "domain_controller"],
    22 |                 "description": "Pre-auth SSRF and Remote Code Execution on Microsoft Exchange servers."
    23 |             },
    24 |             {
    25 |                 "incident_name": "PrintNightmare (CVE-2021-34527)",
    26 |                 "attack_chain": ["internal_user", "print_spooler", "domain_controller"],
    27 |                 "description": "Remote code execution in the Windows Print Spooler service for privilege escalation."
    28 |             }
    29 |         ]
    30 | 
    31 |     def validate_path(self, path_nodes):
    32 |         """
    33 |         Validates a simulated attack path against historical breach data.
    34 |         Returns a score (0-1) and the most similar incident.
    35 |         """
    36 |         if not path_nodes:
    37 |             return 0.0, None
    38 | 
    39 |         best_score = 0.0
    40 |         best_match = None
    41 | 
    42 |         # Simple overlap-based scoring for Stage 4
    43 |         for breach in self.breaches:
    44 |             breach_chain = breach["attack_chain"]
    45 |             
    46 |             # Count common nodes
    47 |             common_nodes = set(path_nodes).intersection(set(breach_chain))
    48 |             
    49 |             # Simple Jaccard-like similarity for sequence comparison
    50 |             if not breach_chain:
    51 |                 continue
    52 |                 
    53 |             # Weighted by sequence position similarity (bonus if start/end match)
    54 |             base_overlap = len(common_nodes) / max(len(path_nodes), len(breach_chain))
    55 |             
    56 |             # Position bonus (Entry point match)
    57 |             entry_match = 0.15 if path_nodes[0].lower() == breach_chain[0].lower() else 0.0
    58 |             
    59 |             # Target match (last node)
    60 |             target_match = 0.15 if path_nodes[-1].lower() == breach_chain[-1].lower() else 0.0
    61 |             
    62 |             total_score = min(base_overlap + entry_match + target_match, 1.0)
    63 |             
    64 |             if total_score > best_score:
    65 |                 best_score = total_score
    66 |                 best_match = breach["incident_name"]
    67 | 
    68 |         # Ensure validation_score is None if no meaningful match found (as per fallback requirement)
    69 |         if best_score < 0.2:
    70 |             return None, None
    71 | 
    72 |         return round(best_score, 2), best_match
    73 | 


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: backend/vulnerability_intelligence.py
LINES: 176
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | import json
     2 | import gzip
     3 | import logging
     4 | import os
     5 | from typing import Optional, Dict
     6 | 
     7 | # Configure minimal logging
     8 | logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(levelname)s: %(message)s')
     9 | logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    10 | 
    11 | class VulnerabilityIntelligence:
    12 |     """
    13 |     Standalone module for CVE vulnerability intelligence using NVD 2.0 dataset.
    14 |     Optimized for performance and memory efficiency.
    15 |     """
    16 | 
    17 |     def __init__(self, nvd_file_path: str, lazy_load: bool = True):
    18 |         self.nvd_file_path = nvd_file_path
    19 |         self.lazy_load = lazy_load
    20 |         self.cve_cache: Dict[str, dict] = {}
    21 |         self._is_loaded = False
    22 | 
    23 |         if not self.lazy_load:
    24 |             self._load_dataset()
    25 | 
    26 |     def _load_dataset(self) -> None:
    27 |         """
    28 |         Parses the NVD 2.0 dataset from the provided JSON or JSON.GZ file.
    29 |         Builds an in-memory index/cache of structured intelligence.
    30 |         """
    31 |         if self._is_loaded:
    32 |             return
    33 | 
    34 |         if not os.path.exists(self.nvd_file_path):
    35 |             logger.error(f"NVD dataset file not found: {self.nvd_file_path}")
    36 |             return
    37 | 
    38 |         try:
    39 |             logger.info(f"Loading NVD dataset from {self.nvd_file_path}...")
    40 |             
    41 |             # Auto-detect gzip by extension or file signature
    42 |             if self.nvd_file_path.endswith('.gz'):
    43 |                 with gzip.open(self.nvd_file_path, 'rt', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    44 |                     data = json.load(f)
    45 |             else:
    46 |                 with open(self.nvd_file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    47 |                     data = json.load(f)
    48 | 
    49 |             if "vulnerabilities" not in data:
    50 |                 logger.error("Invalid NVD 2.0 dataset format: missing 'vulnerabilities' key.")
    51 |                 return
    52 | 
    53 |             self._parse_vulnerabilities(data["vulnerabilities"])
    54 |             self._is_loaded = True
    55 |             logger.info(f"Successfully loaded and indexed {len(self.cve_cache)} CVEs.")
    56 | 
    57 |         except (json.JSONDecodeError, IOError, gzip.BadGzipFile) as e:
    58 |             logger.error(f"Failed to load NVD dataset: {str(e)}")
    59 |         except Exception as e:
    60 |             logger.error(f"Unexpected error during dataset loading: {str(e)}")
    61 | 
    62 |     def _parse_vulnerabilities(self, vulnerabilities: list) -> None:
    63 |         """
    64 |         Iterates through vulnerabilities and extracts structured intelligence.
    65 |         """
    66 |         for entry in vulnerabilities:
    67 |             cve = entry.get("cve")
    68 |             if not cve:
    69 |                 continue
    70 | 
    71 |             cve_id = cve.get("id")
    72 |             if not cve_id:
    73 |                 continue
    74 | 
    75 |             metrics = cve.get("metrics", {})
    76 |             
    77 |             # Try CVSS v3.1 first, then v3.0
    78 |             cvss_metric = None
    79 |             if "cvssMetricV31" in metrics and metrics["cvssMetricV31"]:
    80 |                 cvss_metric = metrics["cvssMetricV31"][0]
    81 |             elif "cvssMetricV30" in metrics and metrics["cvssMetricV30"]:
    82 |                 cvss_metric = metrics["cvssMetricV30"][0]
    83 | 
    84 |             if not cvss_metric:
    85 |                 continue
    86 | 
    87 |             cvss_data = cvss_metric.get("cvssData")
    88 |             if not cvss_data:
    89 |                 continue
    90 | 
    91 |             # Extract fields
    92 |             base_score = cvss_data.get("baseScore")
    93 |             exploitability_score = cvss_metric.get("exploitabilityScore")
    94 |             
    95 |             # Ensure we have at least the base score
    96 |             if base_score is None:
    97 |                 continue
    98 | 
    99 |             intelligence = {
   100 |                 "cve_id": cve_id,
   101 |                 "base_score": float(base_score),
   102 |                 "exploitability_score": float(exploitability_score) if exploitability_score is not None else 0.0,
   103 |                 "attack_vector": cvss_data.get("attackVector"),
   104 |                 "attack_complexity": cvss_data.get("attackComplexity"),
   105 |                 "privileges_required": cvss_data.get("privilegesRequired"),
   106 |                 "user_interaction": cvss_data.get("userInteraction"),
   107 |                 "scope": cvss_data.get("scope"),
   108 |                 "normalized_exploitability": (float(exploitability_score) / 10.0) if exploitability_score is not None else 0.0
   109 |             }
   110 | 
   111 |             # Cache only the required structured data to save memory
   112 |             self.cve_cache[cve_id] = intelligence
   113 | 
   114 |     def get_cve_intelligence(self, cve_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
   115 |         """
   116 |         Retrieves structured intelligence for a given CVE ID.
   117 |         Returns None if not found or if metrics are missing.
   118 |         """
   119 |         if self.lazy_load and not self._is_loaded:
   120 |             self._load_dataset()
   121 | 
   122 |         return self.cve_cache.get(cve_id)
   123 | 
   124 | if __name__ == "__main__":
   125 |     # Test Block for verification
   126 |     import tempfile
   127 |     
   128 |     # Create a mock NVD 2.0 JSON file for testing
   129 |     mock_data = {
   130 |         "vulnerabilities": [
   131 |             {
   132 |                 "cve": {
   133 |                     "id": "CVE-2024-3094",
   134 |                     "metrics": {
   135 |                         "cvssMetricV31": [
   136 |                             {
   137 |                                 "cvssData": {
   138 |                                     "baseScore": 10.0,
   139 |                                     "attackVector": "NETWORK",
   140 |                                     "attackComplexity": "LOW",
   141 |                                     "privilegesRequired": "NONE",
   142 |                                     "userInteraction": "NONE",
   143 |                                     "scope": "CHANGED"
   144 |                                 },
   145 |                                 "exploitabilityScore": 3.9
   146 |                             }
   147 |                         ]
   148 |                     }
   149 |                 }
   150 |             }
   151 |         ]
   152 |     }
   153 | 
   154 |     with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.json', delete=False) as tmp:
   155 |         json.dump(mock_data, tmp)
   156 |         tmp_path = tmp.name
   157 | 
   158 |     try:
   159 |         vi = VulnerabilityIntelligence(nvd_file_path=tmp_path, lazy_load=True)
   160 |         intel = vi.get_cve_intelligence("CVE-2024-3094")
   161 |         
   162 |         if intel:
   163 |             print("Successfully retrieved CVE intelligence:")
   164 |             import pprint
   165 |             pprint.pprint(intel)
   166 |         else:
   167 |             print("Failed to retrieve CVE intelligence.")
   168 |             
   169 |         # Test missing CVE
   170 |         missing_intel = vi.get_cve_intelligence("CVE-1999-9999")
   171 |         print(f"Querying missing CVE: {missing_intel}")
   172 |         
   173 |     finally:
   174 |         if os.path.exists(tmp_path):
   175 |             os.remove(tmp_path)
   176 | 


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: backend/mitre_mapper.py
LINES: 81
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | from typing import List, Optional
     2 | 
     3 | class MitreMapper:
     4 |     """
     5 |     Modular mapping layer between CVE intelligence and MITRE ATT&CK techniques.
     6 |     Rule-based, deterministic, and lightweight.
     7 |     """
     8 | 
     9 |     def __init__(self):
    10 |         # Hardcoded minimal dictionary for requested techniques
    11 |         self.TECHNIQUES = {
    12 |             "T1190": {
    13 |                 "technique_id": "T1190",
    14 |                 "technique_name": "Exploit Public-Facing Application",
    15 |                 "tactic": "Initial Access"
    16 |             },
    17 |             "T1068": {
    18 |                 "technique_id": "T1068",
    19 |                 "technique_name": "Exploitation for Privilege Escalation",
    20 |                 "tactic": "Privilege Escalation"
    21 |             },
    22 |             "T1021": {
    23 |                 "technique_id": "T1021",
    24 |                 "technique_name": "Remote Services",
    25 |                 "tactic": "Lateral Movement"
    26 |             },
    27 |             "T1041": {
    28 |                 "technique_id": "T1041",
    29 |                 "technique_name": "Exfiltration Over C2 Channel",
    30 |                 "tactic": "Exfiltration"
    31 |             }
    32 |         }
    33 | 
    34 |     def map_cve_to_techniques(
    35 |         self, 
    36 |         cve_intel: dict, 
    37 |         is_priv_esc: bool = False, 
    38 |         is_lateral_movement: bool = False, 
    39 |         is_high_value_access: bool = False
    40 |     ) -> List[dict]:
    41 |         """
    42 |         Maps CVE characteristics and path context to MITRE ATT&CK techniques.
    43 |         """
    44 |         mapped_techniques = []
    45 | 
    46 |         # Rule 1: NETWORK attack vector -> T1190
    47 |         if cve_intel.get("attack_vector") == "NETWORK":
    48 |             mapped_techniques.append(self.TECHNIQUES["T1190"])
    49 | 
    50 |         # Rule 2: LOW or NONE privileges required + Privilege Escalation -> T1068
    51 |         privs = cve_intel.get("privileges_required")
    52 |         if privs in ("LOW", "NONE") and is_priv_esc:
    53 |             mapped_techniques.append(self.TECHNIQUES["T1068"])
    54 | 
    55 |         # Rule 3: Node-to-node movement -> T1021
    56 |         if is_lateral_movement:
    57 |             mapped_techniques.append(self.TECHNIQUES["T1021"])
    58 | 
    59 |         # Rule 4: Data access on high-value node -> T1041
    60 |         if is_high_value_access:
    61 |             mapped_techniques.append(self.TECHNIQUES["T1041"])
    62 | 
    63 |         return mapped_techniques
    64 | 
    65 | if __name__ == "__main__":
    66 |     # Test Block for verification
    67 |     mapper = MitreMapper()
    68 |     
    69 |     # Test 1: Network RCE CVE
    70 |     mock_cve = {"attack_vector": "NETWORK", "privileges_required": "NONE"}
    71 |     print(f"Test 1: {mapper.map_cve_to_techniques(mock_cve)}")
    72 |     
    73 |     # Test 2: Privilege escalation path
    74 |     print(f"Test 2: {mapper.map_cve_to_techniques(mock_cve, is_priv_esc=True)}")
    75 |     
    76 |     # Test 3: Lateral movement
    77 |     print(f"Test 3: {mapper.map_cve_to_techniques({}, is_lateral_movement=True)}")
    78 |     
    79 |     # Test 4: High-value access
    80 |     print(f"Test 4: {mapper.map_cve_to_techniques({}, is_high_value_access=True)}")
    81 |

     7 | const DEMO_SCENARIO_LIST = [
     8 |     "Public Web Breach",
     9 |     "Zero-Trust Segmented Network",
    10 |     "Dual Entry Intrusion",
    11 |     "Exposed Database Crisis",

    75 |                     Run Simulation
    76 |                 </button>
    77 | 
    78 |                 {/* Playback Controls */}
    79 |                 {hasAttackPath && (
    80 |                     <div className="flex items-center gap-[3px] border-l border-[#E5E5E5] pl-[8px]">
    81 |                         {/* Play / Pause Toggle */}
    82 |                         {isPlaying ? (

    84 |                                 onClick={pausePlayback}
    85 |                                 title="Pause Playback"

    87 |                             >

    89 |                                 Pause
    90 |                             </button>
    91 |                         ) : (

    96 |                             >
    97 |                                 <Play size={11} className="fill-current" />
    98 |                                 {attackPlaybackStep < 0 ? 'Play' : playbackFinished ? 'Play' : 'Resume'}
    99 |                             </button>
   100 |                         )}
   101 | 
   102 |                         {/* Replay */}
   103 |                         <button
   104 |                             onClick={replayPlayback}
   105 |                             title="Replay"
   106 |                             className="inline-flex items-center gap-[4px] text-[11px] font-medium text-[#444] hover:text-[#111] transition-colors whitespace-nowrap px-[4px] py-[2px] rounded-[6px] hover:bg-black/[0.04]"

FILE: frontend/src/components/Nodes/InfrastructureNode.tsx

     1 | 'use client';
     2 | 
     3 | import React, { memo } from 'react';
     4 | import { Handle, Position, NodeProps } from '@xyflow/react';
     5 | import {
     6 |   Server,
     7 |   Database,
     8 |   Shield,
     9 |   Cloud,
    10 |   Monitor,
    11 |   HardDrive,
    12 |   Cpu,
    13 |   Router,
    14 |   Split,
    15 |   ShieldCheck,
    16 |   Eye,
    17 |   Activity,
    18 |   Laptop,
    19 |   Radio,
    20 |   Globe,
    21 |   Mail,
    22 |   Printer as PrinterIcon,
    23 |   ArrowLeftRight,
    24 |   Waypoints,
    25 |   KeyRound,
    26 | } from 'lucide-react';
    27 | import { useNodeStateStore } from '../../modules/nodeState';
    28 | import { calculateNodeRiskCategory } from '../../utils/calculateNodeRisk';
    29 | 
    30 | export type InfrastructureNodeType =
    31 |   | 'WebServer'
    32 |   | 'AppServer'
    33 |   | 'Database'
    34 |   | 'Firewall'
    35 |   | 'Storage'
    36 |   | 'CloudInstance'
    37 |   | 'Workstation'
    38 |   | 'Router'
    39 |   | 'LoadBalancer'
    40 |   | 'VPN'
    41 |   | 'IDS'
    42 |   | 'SIEM'
    43 |   | 'Endpoint'
    44 |   | 'IoTDevice'
    45 |   | 'DNSServer'
    46 |   | 'SMTPServer'
    47 |   | 'Printer'
    48 |   | 'ProxyServer'
    49 |   | 'APIGateway'
    50 |   | 'AuthServer';
    51 | 
    52 | export interface InfrastructureNodeData {
    53 |   label: string;
    54 |   type: InfrastructureNodeType;
    55 |   ports?: string[];
    56 |   vulnerabilities?: number;
    57 |   status?: 'online' | 'compromised' | 'offline';
    58 |   ip?: string;
    59 |   publicExposure: boolean;
    60 |   privilegeLevel: 'Low' | 'Medium' | 'High';
    61 |   vulnerabilitiesList: string[];
    62 |   dataCriticality: 'Low' | 'Medium' | 'High';
    63 |   highlighted?: boolean;
    64 |   compromised?: boolean;
    65 |   isEntry?: boolean;
    66 |   isTarget?: boolean;
    67 |   attackStep?: number;
    68 | }
    69 | 
    70 | const getIcon = (type: InfrastructureNodeType, isCompromised: boolean) => {
    71 |   const colorClass = isCompromised ? 'text-[#DC2626]' : (() => {
    72 |     switch (type) {
    73 |       case 'WebServer': return 'text-blue-500';
    74 |       case 'AppServer': return 'text-purple-500';
    75 |       case 'Database': return 'text-orange-500';
    76 |       case 'Storage': return 'text-teal-500';
    77 |       case 'Firewall': return 'text-gray-500';
    78 |       case 'CloudInstance': return 'text-indigo-500';
    79 |       case 'Workstation': return 'text-slate-500';
    80 |       case 'DNSServer': return 'text-cyan-500';
    81 |       case 'SMTPServer': return 'text-rose-500';
    82 |       case 'Printer': return 'text-gray-400';
    83 |       case 'ProxyServer': return 'text-amber-500';
    84 |       case 'APIGateway': return 'text-violet-500';
    85 |       case 'AuthServer': return 'text-emerald-500';
    86 |       default: return 'text-gray-500';
    87 |     }
    88 |   })();
    89 | 
    90 |   const iconProps = { className: `w-4 h-4 ${colorClass}` };
    91 | 
    92 |   switch (type) {
    93 |     case 'WebServer': return <Server {...iconProps} />;
    94 |     case 'AppServer': return <Cpu {...iconProps} />;
    95 |     case 'Database': return <Database {...iconProps} />;
    96 |     case 'Firewall': return <Shield {...iconProps} />;
    97 |     case 'CloudInstance': return <Cloud {...iconProps} />;
    98 |     case 'Workstation': return <Monitor {...iconProps} />;
    99 |     case 'Storage': return <HardDrive {...iconProps} />;
   100 |     case 'Router': return <Router {...iconProps} />;
   101 |     case 'LoadBalancer': return <Split {...iconProps} />;
   102 |     case 'VPN': return <ShieldCheck {...iconProps} />;
   103 |     case 'IDS': return <Eye {...iconProps} />;
   104 |     case 'SIEM': return <Activity {...iconProps} />;
   105 |     case 'Endpoint': return <Laptop {...iconProps} />;
   106 |     case 'IoTDevice': return <Radio {...iconProps} />;
   107 |     case 'DNSServer': return <Globe {...iconProps} />;
   108 |     case 'SMTPServer': return <Mail {...iconProps} />;
   109 |     case 'Printer': return <PrinterIcon {...iconProps} />;
   110 |     case 'ProxyServer': return <ArrowLeftRight {...iconProps} />;
   111 |     case 'APIGateway': return <Waypoints {...iconProps} />;
   112 |     case 'AuthServer': return <KeyRound {...iconProps} />;
   113 |     default: return <Server {...iconProps} />;
   114 |   }
   115 | };
   116 | 
   117 | const InfrastructureNode = ({ id, data, selected }: NodeProps<{ data: InfrastructureNodeData } & any>) => {
   118 |   const {
   119 |     label,
   120 |     type,
   121 |     ports,
   122 |     ip,
   123 |     compromised,
   124 |     isEntry,
   125 |     isTarget,
   126 |     attackStep
   127 |   } = data;
   128 | 
   129 |   const pendingConnectionNodeId = useNodeStateStore((s) => s.pendingConnectionNodeId);
   130 |   const hoveredTimelineNodeId = useNodeStateStore((s) => s.hoveredTimelineNodeId);
   131 |   const activeMitigations = useNodeStateStore((s) => s.activeMitigations);
   132 |   const heatmapEnabled = useNodeStateStore((s) => s.heatmapEnabled);
   133 |   const attackPath = useNodeStateStore((s) => s.attackPath);
   134 |   const attackPlaybackStep = useNodeStateStore((s) => s.attackPlaybackStep);
   135 |   const isPlaying = useNodeStateStore((s) => s.isPlaying);
   136 | 
   137 |   const isConnectionSource = pendingConnectionNodeId === id;
   138 |   const isConnectionTarget = !!pendingConnectionNodeId && pendingConnectionNodeId !== id;
   139 |   const isTimelineHovered = hoveredTimelineNodeId === id;
   140 |   const isCompromised = compromised;
   141 |   const isMitigated = activeMitigations.some(m => m.affectedNodes?.includes(id));
   142 | 
   143 |   // Playback-specific state
   144 |   const pathIndex = attackPath.indexOf(id);
   145 |   const isInPath = pathIndex !== -1;
   146 |   const isPlaybackActive = attackPlaybackStep >= 0 && isInPath;
   147 |   const isCurrentlyReached = isPlaybackActive && pathIndex <= attackPlaybackStep;
   148 |   const isCurrentStep = isPlaybackActive && pathIndex === attackPlaybackStep;
   149 |   const isPlaybackEntry = isCurrentlyReached && pathIndex === 0;
   150 |   const isPlaybackTarget = isCurrentlyReached && pathIndex === attackPath.length - 1;
   151 |   const isPlaybackIntermediate = isCurrentlyReached && !isPlaybackEntry && !isPlaybackTarget;
   152 | 
   153 |   const riskCategory = calculateNodeRiskCategory(data);
   154 | 
   155 |   const hoverDetails = `${label}
   156 | IP: ${ip || 'N/A'}
   157 | Ports: ${ports && ports.length > 0 ? ports.join(', ') : 'None'}
   158 | Privilege: ${data.privilegeLevel}
   159 | Asset Value: ${data.assetValue || 3}
   160 | Exposure: ${data.publicExposure ? 'Public' : 'Internal'}
   161 | Risk Level: ${riskCategory}`;
   162 | 
   163 |   // Determine playback-specific styling
   164 |   const getPlaybackClass = () => {
   165 |     if (!isPlaybackActive || !isCurrentlyReached) return '';
   166 |     if (isPlaybackEntry) return 'playback-entry-pulse';
   167 |     if (isPlaybackTarget) return 'playback-target-pulse';
   168 |     if (isPlaybackIntermediate) return 'playback-intermediate';
   169 |     return '';
   170 |   };
   171 | 
   172 |   const getPlaybackBorderStyle = (): React.CSSProperties => {
   173 |     if (!isPlaybackActive) return {};
   174 |     if (!isCurrentlyReached) return { opacity: 0.35, transition: 'opacity 0.3s ease' };
   175 |     if (isPlaybackTarget) return {
   176 |       borderColor: '#DC2626',
   177 |       boxShadow: '0 0 20px rgba(220,38,38,0.4), 0 0 40px rgba(220,38,38,0.15)',
   178 |       transition: 'all 0.3s ease',
   179 |     };
   180 |     if (isPlaybackEntry) return {
   181 |       borderColor: '#DC2626',
   182 |       boxShadow: '0 0 15px rgba(220,38,38,0.35), 0 0 30px rgba(220,38,38,0.1)',
   183 |       transition: 'all 0.3s ease',
   184 |     };
   185 |     if (isPlaybackIntermediate) return {
   186 |       borderColor: '#DC2626',
   187 |       boxShadow: '0 0 8px rgba(220,38,38,0.2)',
   188 |       transition: 'all 0.3s ease',
   189 |     };
   190 |     return {};
   191 |   };
   192 | 
   193 |   return (
   194 |     <div
   195 |       title={hoverDetails}
   196 |       className={`
   197 |       relative min-w-[170px] max-w-[220px] bg-white rounded-lg p-3
   198 |       transition-all duration-200 cursor-pointer
   199 |       ${getPlaybackClass()}
   200 |       ${heatmapEnabled
   201 |           ? riskCategory === 'CRITICAL' ? 'border-4' : ['HIGH', 'MEDIUM'].includes(riskCategory) ? 'border-2' : 'border'
   202 |           : 'border'}
   203 |       ${isConnectionSource
   204 |           ? 'border-[#111] shadow-md ring-2 ring-[#111]/10'
   205 |           : selected || isTimelineHovered ? 'border-[#111] shadow-md -translate-y-[1px]' : 'border-[#E5E5E5]'}
   206 |       ${isConnectionTarget
   207 |           ? 'border-[#6B7280] shadow-sm ring-1 ring-[#6B7280]/20 hover:border-[#111] hover:ring-[#111]/20 hover:shadow-md'
   208 |           : ''}
   209 |       ${isTarget && !isPlaybackActive
   210 |           ? '!border-[#DC2626] shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(220,38,38,0.2)]'
   211 |           : ''}
   212 |       ${isCompromised && !isTarget && !isPlaybackActive
   213 |           ? 'border-[#DC2626] bg-red-50'
   214 |           : !isConnectionSource && !isConnectionTarget && !selected && !isTimelineHovered && !isPlaybackActive
   215 |             ? 'hover:border-[#888] hover:shadow-[0_2px_8px_rgba(0,0,0,0.08)] hover:-translate-y-[1px]'
   216 |             : ''}
   217 |       ${isEntry && !selected && !isTimelineHovered && !isPlaybackActive
   218 |           ? 'ring-2 ring-red-500 ring-offset-2 shadow-[0_0_12px_rgba(239,68,68,0.6)]'
   219 |           : ''}
   220 |     `}
   221 |       style={getPlaybackBorderStyle()}
   222 |     >
   223 |       {/* Connection mode indicator */}
   224 |       {isConnectionSource && (
   225 |         <div className="absolute -top-2 -right-2 w-4 h-4 bg-[#111] rounded-full flex items-center justify-center z-10">
   226 |           <div className="w-1.5 h-1.5 bg-white rounded-full" />
   227 |         </div>
   228 |       )}
   229 |       {isConnectionTarget && (
   230 |         <div className="absolute -top-1.5 -right-1.5 w-3 h-3 bg-[#6B7280] rounded-full opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity z-10" />
   231 |       )}
   232 | 
   233 |       {/* Badges */}
   234 |       {isEntry && (
   235 |         <div className="absolute -top-2.5 -left-2.5 bg-red-500 text-white text-[9px] font-bold px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded shadow-sm z-10 pointer-events-none">
   236 |           ENTRY
   237 |         </div>
   238 |       )}
   239 |       {isTarget && (
   240 |         <div className="absolute -bottom-2.5 -right-2.5 bg-[#DC2626] text-white text-[9px] font-bold px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded shadow-sm z-10 pointer-events-none">
   241 |           TARGET
   242 |         </div>
   243 |       )}
   244 |       {attackStep !== undefined && (
   245 |         <div className="absolute -top-2.5 -right-2.5 w-[20px] h-[20px] bg-white border-2 border-[#111] text-[#111] text-[11px] font-bold rounded-full flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm z-10 pointer-events-none">
   246 |           {attackStep}
   247 |         </div>
   248 |       )}
   249 |       {isMitigated && (
   250 |         <div className="absolute -bottom-2 -left-2 bg-emerald-100 border border-emerald-300 text-emerald-700 text-[10px] flex items-center gap-1 font-bold px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-md shadow-sm z-10 pointer-events-none">
   251 |           <Shield size={10} />
   252 |           Patched
   253 |         </div>
   254 |       )}
   255 | 
   256 |       {/* Playback step indicator */}
   257 |       {isCurrentStep && isPlaybackActive && (
   258 |         <div className="absolute -top-3 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 bg-[#DC2626] text-white text-[8px] font-bold px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full shadow-lg z-20 pointer-events-none whitespace-nowrap playback-step-badge">
   259 |           STEP {pathIndex + 1}
   260 |         </div>
   261 |       )}
   262 | 
   263 |       {/* Top Handle */}
   264 |       <Handle
   265 |         type="target"
   266 |         position={Position.Top}
   267 |         className="!w-3 !h-3 !bg-[#D1D5DB] !border-2 !border-white"
   268 |       />
   269 | 
   270 |       <div className="flex items-start gap-2.5">
   271 |         <div className={`
   272 |           p-1.5 rounded-md transition-colors
   273 |           ${isCompromised || isCurrentlyReached
   274 |             ? 'bg-red-100'
   275 |             : isConnectionSource
   276 |               ? 'bg-[#F0F0F0]'
   277 |               : 'bg-[#F7F7F7]'}
   278 |         `}>
   279 |           {getIcon(type, !!(isCompromised || isCurrentlyReached))}
   280 |         </div>
   281 | 
   282 |         <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
   283 |           <div className="text-[10px] text-[#9CA3AF] uppercase tracking-wider mb-0.5">
   284 |             {type.replace(/([A-Z])/g, ' $1').trim()}
   285 |           </div>
   286 |           <div className={`text-[13px] font-semibold truncate ${isCompromised || isCurrentlyReached ? 'text-[#DC2626]' : 'text-[#111]'}`}>
   287 |             {label}
   288 |           </div>
   289 | 
   290 |           <div className="mt-1 flex gap-1 flex-wrap">
   291 |             {data.publicExposure && (
   292 |               <span className="text-[10px] font-medium border px-1 rounded text-black border-gray-400">
   293 |                 [ENTRY]
   294 |               </span>
   295 |             )}
   296 |             {(data.assetValue || 0) >= 8 && (
   297 |               <span className="text-[10px] font-medium border px-1 rounded text-black border-gray-400">
   298 |                 [CRITICAL]
   299 |               </span>
   300 |             )}
   301 |             {heatmapEnabled && (
   302 |               <span className="text-xs border rounded px-1 text-black border-gray-400">
   303 |                 RISK: {riskCategory}
   304 |               </span>
   305 |             )}
   306 |           </div>
   307 | 
   308 |           <div className="mt-1.5 space-y-0.5">
   309 |             {ip && (
   310 |               <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
   311 |                 <span className="text-[10px] text-[#D1D5DB]">IP</span>
   312 |                 <span className="text-[11px] text-[#6B7280] font-mono">{ip}</span>
   313 |               </div>
   314 |             )}
   315 |             {ports && ports.length > 0 && (
   316 |               <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
   317 |                 <span className="text-[10px] text-[#D1D5DB]">Ports</span>
   318 |                 <span className="text-[11px] text-[#6B7280] font-mono">{ports.length}</span>
   319 |               </div>
   320 |             )}
   321 |           </div>
   322 |         </div>
   323 |       </div>
   324 | 
   325 |       {/* Status Dot */}
   326 |       <div className={`
   327 |         absolute top-3 right-3 w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full
   328 |         ${isCompromised || isCurrentlyReached ? 'bg-[#DC2626]' :
   329 |           data.status === 'online' ? 'bg-emerald-500' : 'bg-[#D1D5DB]'}
   330 |       `} />
   331 | 
   332 |       {/* Bottom Handle */}
   333 |       <Handle
   334 |         type="source"
   335 |         position={Position.Bottom}
   336 |         className="!w-3 !h-3 !bg-[#9CA3AF] !border-2 !border-white"
   337 |       />
   338 |     </div>
   339 |   );
   340 | };
   341 | 
   342 | export default memo(InfrastructureNode);
   343 |
