────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: frontend/src/modules/defender/chokePointAnalysis.ts
LINES: 38
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | 
     2 | import { Edge } from '@xyflow/react';
     3 | import { InfrastructureNode } from '../nodeState';
     4 | 
     5 | export interface NodeCentrality {
     6 |   nodeId: string;
     7 |   count: number;
     8 |   score: number; // 0 to 1
     9 | }
    10 | 
    11 | /**
    12 |  * Identifies nodes that appear most frequently across all attack paths.
    13 |  * These are "choke points" where a single fix can break multiple paths.
    14 |  */
    15 | export const calculateChokePoints = (
    16 |   nodes: InfrastructureNode[],
    17 |   attackPaths: string[][]
    18 | ): NodeCentrality[] => {
    19 |   const nodeFrequencies: Record<string, number> = {};
    20 | 
    21 |   attackPaths.forEach(path => {
    22 |     path.forEach(nodeId => {
    23 |       nodeFrequencies[nodeId] = (nodeFrequencies[nodeId] || 0) + 1;
    24 |     });
    25 |   });
    26 | 
    27 |   const maxFrequency = Math.max(...Object.values(nodeFrequencies), 0);
    28 |   
    29 |   return nodes.map(node => {
    30 |     const count = nodeFrequencies[node.id] || 0;
    31 |     return {
    32 |       nodeId: node.id,
    33 |       count,
    34 |       score: maxFrequency > 0 ? count / maxFrequency : 0
    35 |     };
    36 |   }).sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count);
    37 | };
    38 | 


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: frontend/src/modules/defender/mitigationSimulator.ts
LINES: 127
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | 
     2 | import { Edge } from '@xyflow/react';
     3 | import { InfrastructureNode } from '../nodeState';
     4 | import { Recommendation } from './recommendationEngine';
     5 | import { getInfrastructureAttackPaths } from './pathAnalysis';
     6 | 
     7 | export interface MitigationResult {
     8 |   riskBefore: number;
     9 |   riskAfter: number;
    10 |   riskReduction: number;
    11 |   pathDelta: number;
    12 |   newNodes: InfrastructureNode[];
    13 |   newEdges: Edge[];
    14 | }
    15 | 
    16 | /**
    17 |  * Simulates applying multiple mitigations to the current graph.
    18 |  */
    19 | export const simulateMitigation = (
    20 |   nodes: InfrastructureNode[],
    21 |   edges: Edge[],
    22 |   recommendations: Recommendation[],
    23 |   currentRiskScore: number
    24 | ): MitigationResult => {
    25 |   let updatedNodes = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(nodes)) as InfrastructureNode[];
    26 |   let updatedEdges = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(edges)) as Edge[];
    27 | 
    28 |   const pathsBefore = getInfrastructureAttackPaths(nodes, edges);
    29 | 
    30 |   recommendations.forEach(recommendation => {
    31 |     switch (recommendation.type) {
    32 |       case 'PATCH_VULNERABILITY':
    33 |       case 'SERVICE_HARDENING': {
    34 |         const { nodeId, vulnerability } = recommendation.actionPayload;
    35 |         const node = updatedNodes.find(n => n.id === nodeId);
    36 |         if (node) {
    37 |           if (vulnerability) {
    38 |             node.data.vulnerabilitiesList = node.data.vulnerabilitiesList.filter(v => v !== vulnerability);
    39 |             node.data.vulnerabilities = node.data.vulnerabilitiesList.length;
    40 |           }
    41 | 
    42 |           // Structural changes to node ports
    43 |           const desc = recommendation.description.toLowerCase();
    44 |           if (desc.includes('database') || desc.includes('db')) {
    45 |             node.data.ports = node.data.ports?.filter(p => !['3306', '5432', '27017'].includes(p)) || [];
    46 |           } else if (desc.includes('ssh') || desc.includes('port 22')) {
    47 |             node.data.ports = node.data.ports?.filter(p => p !== '22') || [];
    48 |           } else if (desc.includes('web') || desc.includes('http')) {
    49 |             node.data.ports = node.data.ports?.filter(p => !['80', '443'].includes(p)) || [];
    50 |           } else if (desc.includes('restrict')) {
    51 |             node.data.ports = [];
    52 |           }
    53 | 
    54 |           if (desc.includes('public') || desc.includes('web')) {
    55 |             node.data.publicExposure = false;
    56 |           }
    57 | 
    58 |           node.data.compromised = false;
    59 | 
    60 |           // ── ATTACK PATH COLLAPSE ──────────────────────────────────
    61 |           // Remove ALL inbound edges to the patched node.
    62 |           // This simulates firewall-blocking: attackers can no longer
    63 |           // reach this node, so every attack path traversing it is
    64 |           // structurally eliminated from the graph.
    65 |           updatedEdges = updatedEdges.filter(
    66 |             edge => edge.target !== nodeId
    67 |           );
    68 |         }
    69 |         break;
    70 |       }
    71 |       case 'NETWORK_SEGMENTATION': {
    72 |         const { edgeId } = recommendation.actionPayload;
    73 | 
    74 |         // ── SEGMENTATION LOGIC ──────────────────────────────────────
    75 |         // Primary: remove by edgeId
    76 |         const edgeBefore = updatedEdges.length;
    77 |         updatedEdges = updatedEdges.filter(e => e.id !== edgeId);
    78 | 
    79 |         // Fallback: if edgeId didn't match, try source/target from
    80 |         // affectedNodes ([source, target])
    81 |         if (updatedEdges.length === edgeBefore && recommendation.affectedNodes?.length === 2) {
    82 |           const [source, target] = recommendation.affectedNodes;
    83 |           updatedEdges = updatedEdges.filter(
    84 |             edge => !(edge.source === source && edge.target === target)
    85 |           );
    86 |         }
    87 |         break;
    88 |       }
    89 |       case 'PRIVILEGE_RESTRICTION': {
    90 |         const { nodeId, newLevel } = recommendation.actionPayload;
    91 |         const node = updatedNodes.find(n => n.id === nodeId);
    92 |         if (node) {
    93 |           node.data.privilegeLevel = newLevel;
    94 |         }
    95 |         break;
    96 |       }
    97 |     }
    98 |   });
    99 | 
   100 |   const pathsAfter = getInfrastructureAttackPaths(updatedNodes, updatedEdges);
   101 |   const pathDelta = pathsAfter.length - pathsBefore.length;
   102 | 
   103 |   // Calculate new risk score based on path reduction and all mitigations combined
   104 |   let totalEstimatedReduction = 0;
   105 |   recommendations.forEach(r => totalEstimatedReduction += r.estimatedRiskReduction);
   106 |   totalEstimatedReduction = Math.min(totalEstimatedReduction, 95);
   107 | 
   108 |   const riskReductionFactor = pathsBefore.length > 0
   109 |     ? (pathsBefore.length - pathsAfter.length) / pathsBefore.length
   110 |     : totalEstimatedReduction / 100;
   111 | 
   112 |   const actualRiskReduction = Math.max(0.1, riskReductionFactor) * 100;
   113 |   const newRiskScore = recommendations.length > 0
   114 |     ? Math.max(0, currentRiskScore - (currentRiskScore * (actualRiskReduction / 100)))
   115 |     : currentRiskScore;
   116 | 
   117 |   return {
   118 |     riskBefore: currentRiskScore,
   119 |     riskAfter: Math.round(newRiskScore),
   120 |     riskReduction: Math.round(actualRiskReduction),
   121 |     pathDelta,
   122 |     newNodes: updatedNodes,
   123 |     newEdges: updatedEdges
   124 |   };
   125 | };
   126 | 
   127 | 


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: frontend/src/modules/defender/pathAnalysis.ts
LINES: 66
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | 
     2 | import { Edge } from '@xyflow/react';
     3 | import { InfrastructureNode } from '../nodeState';
     4 | 
     5 | /**
     6 |  * Finds all simple paths from source nodes to a target node in a directed graph.
     7 |  */
     8 | export const findAllPaths = (
     9 |   nodes: InfrastructureNode[],
    10 |   edges: Edge[],
    11 |   sourceIds: string[],
    12 |   targetId: string,
    13 |   maxDepth: number = 5
    14 | ): string[][] => {
    15 |   const paths: string[][] = [];
    16 | 
    17 |   const dfs = (currentNodeId: string, targetId: string, currentPath: string[], visited: Set<string>, depth: number) => {
    18 |     if (depth > maxDepth) return;
    19 |     if (currentNodeId === targetId) {
    20 |       paths.push([...currentPath]);
    21 |       return;
    22 |     }
    23 | 
    24 |     visited.add(currentNodeId);
    25 | 
    26 |     const outgoingEdges = edges.filter(e => e.source === currentNodeId);
    27 |     for (const edge of outgoingEdges) {
    28 |       if (!visited.has(edge.target)) {
    29 |         currentPath.push(edge.target);
    30 |         dfs(edge.target, targetId, currentPath, visited, depth + 1);
    31 |         currentPath.pop();
    32 |       }
    33 |     }
    34 | 
    35 |     visited.delete(currentNodeId);
    36 |   };
    37 | 
    38 |   sourceIds.forEach(sourceId => {
    39 |     dfs(sourceId, targetId, [sourceId], new Set(), 0);
    40 |   });
    41 | 
    42 |   return paths;
    43 | };
    44 | 
    45 | /**
    46 |  * Gets all attack paths in the current infrastructure.
    47 |  * Sources are nodes with publicExposure = true.
    48 |  * Targets are nodes with high dataCriticality.
    49 |  */
    50 | export const getInfrastructureAttackPaths = (
    51 |   nodes: InfrastructureNode[],
    52 |   edges: Edge[]
    53 | ): string[][] => {
    54 |   const publicNodes = nodes.filter(n => n.data.publicExposure).map(n => n.id);
    55 |   const criticalNodes = nodes.filter(n => n.data.dataCriticality === 'High').map(n => n.id);
    56 |   
    57 |   let allPaths: string[][] = [];
    58 |   criticalNodes.forEach(targetId => {
    59 |     const paths = findAllPaths(nodes, edges, publicNodes, targetId);
    60 |     allPaths = [...allPaths, ...paths];
    61 |   });
    62 | 
    63 |   // Deduplicate paths
    64 |   return Array.from(new Set(allPaths.map(p => JSON.stringify(p)))).map(p => JSON.parse(p));
    65 | };
    66 | 


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: frontend/src/modules/defender/recommendationEngine.ts
LINES: 105
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | 
     2 | import { Edge } from '@xyflow/react';
     3 | import { InfrastructureNode } from '../nodeState';
     4 | import { estimateRiskReduction } from './riskReductionEstimator';
     5 | 
     6 | export type RecommendationType =
     7 |   | 'PATCH_VULNERABILITY'
     8 |   | 'NETWORK_SEGMENTATION'
     9 |   | 'PRIVILEGE_RESTRICTION'
    10 |   | 'SERVICE_HARDENING'
    11 |   | 'ACCESS_CONTROL_FIX';
    12 | 
    13 | export interface Recommendation {
    14 |   id: string;
    15 |   type: RecommendationType;
    16 |   description: string;
    17 |   affectedNodes: string[];
    18 |   affectedEdges?: string[];
    19 |   estimatedRiskReduction: number; // 0 to 100
    20 |   actionPayload: any;
    21 | }
    22 | 
    23 | /**
    24 |  * Generates recommendations based on the current graph and attack paths.
    25 |  */
    26 | export const generateRecommendations = (
    27 |   nodes: InfrastructureNode[],
    28 |   edges: Edge[],
    29 |   attackPaths: string[][]
    30 | ): Recommendation[] => {
    31 |   const recommendations: Recommendation[] = [];
    32 |   const nodeFrequencies: Record<string, number> = {};
    33 | 
    34 |   attackPaths.forEach(path => {
    35 |     path.forEach(nodeId => {
    36 |       nodeFrequencies[nodeId] = (nodeFrequencies[nodeId] || 0) + 1;
    37 |     });
    38 |   });
    39 | 
    40 |   const pathCount = attackPaths.length;
    41 | 
    42 |   nodes.forEach(node => {
    43 |     const freq = nodeFrequencies[node.id] || 0;
    44 |     if (freq === 0) return;
    45 | 
    46 |     // Recommendation for vulnerabilities
    47 |     if (node.data.vulnerabilitiesList.length > 0) {
    48 |       node.data.vulnerabilitiesList.forEach((vuln, index) => {
    49 |         recommendations.push({
    50 |           id: `patch_${node.id}_${index}`,
    51 |           type: 'PATCH_VULNERABILITY',
    52 |           description: `Patch ${node.data.label} ${vuln}`,
    53 |           affectedNodes: [node.id],
    54 |           estimatedRiskReduction: Math.round((freq / pathCount) * 40 + (index === 0 ? 20 : 10)),
    55 |           actionPayload: { nodeId: node.id, vulnerability: vuln }
    56 |         });
    57 |       });
    58 |     }
    59 | 
    60 |     // Recommendation for high privilege
    61 |     if (node.data.privilegeLevel !== 'Low') {
    62 |       recommendations.push({
    63 |         id: `priv_${node.id}`,
    64 |         type: 'PRIVILEGE_RESTRICTION',
    65 |         description: `Restrict privileges on ${node.data.label}`,
    66 |         affectedNodes: [node.id],
    67 |         estimatedRiskReduction: Math.round((freq / pathCount) * 25),
    68 |         actionPayload: { nodeId: node.id, newLevel: 'Low' }
    69 |       });
    70 |     }
    71 | 
    72 |     // Recommendation for segmentation
    73 |     // Find edges where this node is source/target in attack paths
    74 |     edges.forEach(edge => {
    75 |       if (edge.source === node.id || edge.target === node.id) {
    76 |         // Check if this edge is likely part of an attack path
    77 |         const sourceInPath = nodeFrequencies[edge.source] > 0;
    78 |         const targetInPath = nodeFrequencies[edge.target] > 0;
    79 | 
    80 |         if (sourceInPath && targetInPath) {
    81 |           recommendations.push({
    82 |             id: `seg_${edge.id}`,
    83 |             type: 'NETWORK_SEGMENTATION',
    84 |             description: `Isolate ${node.id === edge.source ? 'target' : 'source'} from ${node.data.label}`,
    85 |             affectedNodes: [edge.source, edge.target],
    86 |             affectedEdges: [edge.id],
    87 |             estimatedRiskReduction: Math.round((Math.min(nodeFrequencies[edge.source], nodeFrequencies[edge.target]) / pathCount) * 50),
    88 |             actionPayload: { edgeId: edge.id }
    89 |           });
    90 |         }
    91 |       }
    92 |     });
    93 |   });
    94 | 
    95 |   // Deduplicate and sort by risk reduction
    96 |   const uniqueRecs = recommendations.filter((v, i, a) => a.findIndex(t => t.description === v.description) === i);
    97 | 
    98 |   const finalRecs = uniqueRecs.map(rec => ({
    99 |     ...rec,
   100 |     estimatedRiskReduction: estimateRiskReduction(rec, attackPaths, nodes)
   101 |   }));
   102 | 
   103 |   return finalRecs.sort((a, b) => b.estimatedRiskReduction - a.estimatedRiskReduction).slice(0, 5);
   104 | };
   105 | 


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: frontend/src/modules/defender/riskReductionEstimator.ts
LINES: 47
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | import { InfrastructureNode } from '../nodeState';
     2 | import { Recommendation } from './recommendationEngine';
     3 | 
     4 | export const calculateNodePathFrequency = (nodeId: string, paths: string[][]): number => {
     5 |     if (!paths || paths.length === 0) return 0;
     6 |     const count = paths.filter(p => p.includes(nodeId)).length;
     7 |     return count / paths.length;
     8 | };
     9 | 
    10 | export const estimateExposure = (node: InfrastructureNode): number => {
    11 |     let base = node.data.publicExposure ? 0.6 : 0.1;
    12 |     const ports = node.data.ports || [];
    13 | 
    14 |     if (ports.includes('22')) base += 0.2;
    15 |     if (ports.includes('80') || ports.includes('443')) base += 0.15;
    16 |     if (ports.includes('3306') || ports.includes('5432') || ports.includes('27017')) base += 0.25;
    17 | 
    18 |     return Math.min(1.0, base);
    19 | };
    20 | 
    21 | export const estimateRiskReduction = (
    22 |     recommendation: Recommendation,
    23 |     paths: string[][],
    24 |     nodes: InfrastructureNode[]
    25 | ): number => {
    26 |     let maxReduction = 0;
    27 | 
    28 |     for (const nodeId of recommendation.affectedNodes) {
    29 |         const node = nodes.find(n => n.id === nodeId);
    30 |         if (!node) continue;
    31 | 
    32 |         const nodePathFrequency = calculateNodePathFrequency(nodeId, paths);
    33 |         const nodeExposureScore = estimateExposure(node);
    34 |         const nodeAssetValue = node.data.assetValue || 5;
    35 | 
    36 |         const riskReductionScore = nodePathFrequency * nodeExposureScore * nodeAssetValue;
    37 |         const riskReduction = Math.round(riskReductionScore * 100);
    38 | 
    39 |         if (riskReduction > maxReduction) {
    40 |             maxReduction = riskReduction;
    41 |         }
    42 |     }
    43 | 
    44 |     // Clamp result between 0 and 95
    45 |     return Math.max(0, Math.min(95, maxReduction));
    46 | };
    47 |

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: frontend/src/utils/calculateNodeRisk.ts
LINES: 19
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | export function calculateNodeRiskCategory(nodeData: any): 'LOW' | 'MEDIUM' | 'HIGH' | 'CRITICAL' {
     2 |     const publicExposure = nodeData.publicExposure ? 5 : 0;
     3 | 
     4 |     // Try to use vulnerabilitiesList if available, fallback to numerical vulnerabilities count
     5 |     const vulnsLength = nodeData.vulnerabilitiesList
     6 |         ? nodeData.vulnerabilitiesList.length
     7 |         : (nodeData.vulnerabilities || 0);
     8 | 
     9 |     const assetValue = nodeData.assetValue || 0;
    10 |     const portsLength = nodeData.ports ? nodeData.ports.length : 0;
    11 | 
    12 |     const risk = publicExposure + (vulnsLength * 2) + assetValue + portsLength;
    13 | 
    14 |     if (risk <= 5) return 'LOW';
    15 |     if (risk <= 10) return 'MEDIUM';
    16 |     if (risk <= 15) return 'HIGH';
    17 |     return 'CRITICAL';
    18 | }
    19 |

========================================================================================================================
────  APPENDIX H — API Route Source Code  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
========================================================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: frontend/src/app/api/simulate/route.ts
LINES: 98
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2 | import { spawn } from "child_process";
     3 | import path from "path";
     4 | 
     5 | export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
     6 |   const body = await req.json();
     7 |   const { architecture, attacker_skill, harden_node } = body;
     8 | 
     9 |   if (!architecture) {
    10 |     return NextResponse.json({ error: "Missing architecture field" }, { status: 400 });
    11 |   }
    12 | 
    13 |   // Adjusting backendDir to look outside frontend/ if we're in it.
    14 |   // Assuming process.cwd() is /frontend
    15 |   const isFrontendDir = process.cwd().endsWith("/frontend") || process.cwd().endsWith("\\frontend");
    16 |   const baseProjectDir = isFrontendDir ? path.join(process.cwd(), "..") : process.cwd();
    17 |   const backendDir = path.join(baseProjectDir, "backend");
    18 |   const scriptPath = path.join(backendDir, "main.py");
    19 | 
    20 |   const result = await runPython(scriptPath, backendDir, {
    21 |     architecture,
    22 |     attacker_skill: attacker_skill || 1.0,
    23 |     harden_node: harden_node || null
    24 |   });
    25 | 
    26 | 
    27 | 
    28 |   try {
    29 |     const parsed = JSON.parse(result as string);
    30 |     if (parsed.error || parsed.status === 'error') {
    31 |       return NextResponse.json(parsed, { status: 500 });
    32 |     }
    33 |     return NextResponse.json(parsed);
    34 |   } catch {
    35 |     return NextResponse.json({ error: "Failed to parse simulation output", raw: result }, { status: 500 });
    36 |   }
    37 | }
    38 | 
    39 | 
    40 | function runPython(scriptPath: string, cwd: string, payload: object): Promise<string> {
    41 |   return new Promise<string>((resolve, reject) => {
    42 |     const fs = require("fs");
    43 |     // Fallback chain: venv python → system python3 → system python
    44 |     const venvPython = path.join(cwd, "..", ".venv", "bin", "python3");
    45 |     let command: string;
    46 |     if (process.platform === "win32") {
    47 |       command = "python";
    48 |     } else if (fs.existsSync(venvPython)) {
    49 |       command = venvPython;
    50 |     } else {
    51 |       command = "python3";
    52 |     }
    53 | 
    54 |     const py = spawn(command, [scriptPath], {
    55 |       cwd,
    56 |       env: { ...process.env, PYTHONPATH: cwd },
    57 |     });
    58 | 
    59 |     let stdout = "";
    60 |     let stderr = "";
    61 | 
    62 |     // Timeout: kill after 180 seconds (increased from 60s to accommodate
    63 |     // simulation + RL training + AI explanation pipeline)
    64 |     const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
    65 |       py.kill("SIGKILL");
    66 |       // Return partial results instead of hard rejection
    67 |       if (stdout.trim()) {
    68 |         resolve(stdout);
    69 |       } else {
    70 |         resolve(JSON.stringify({
    71 |           status: "error",
    72 |           error: "Simulation timed out after 180 seconds. Try a smaller graph or disable Monte Carlo.",
    73 |         }));
    74 |       }
    75 |     }, 180000);
    76 | 
    77 |     py.stdout.on("data", (data: Buffer) => { stdout += data.toString(); });
    78 |     py.stderr.on("data", (data: Buffer) => { stderr += data.toString(); });
    79 | 
    80 |     py.on("close", (code: number) => {
    81 |       clearTimeout(timeout);
    82 |       if (code !== 0) {
    83 |         reject(new Error(`Python process exited with code ${code}: ${stderr}`));
    84 |       } else {
    85 |         resolve(stdout);
    86 |       }
    87 |     });
    88 | 
    89 |     py.on("error", (err: Error) => {
    90 |       clearTimeout(timeout);
    91 |       reject(err);
    92 |     });
    93 | 
    94 |     py.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(payload));
    95 |     py.stdin.end();
    96 |   }).catch((err) => JSON.stringify({ error: err.message }));
    97 | }
    98 | 


────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE: frontend/src/app/page.tsx
LINES: 51
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     1 | 'use client';
     2 | 
     3 | import React from 'react';
     4 | import PremiumLayout from '@/layout/PremiumLayout';
     5 | import SimulationWorkspace from '@/components/Canvas/SimulationWorkspace';
     6 | import NodeConfigPanel from '@/components/Panels/NodeConfigPanel';
     7 | 
     8 | import AttackReportView from '@/components/Report/AttackReportView';
     9 | import TopToolbar from '@/components/Toolbar/TopToolbar';
    10 | import FloatingControlBar from '@/components/FloatingControlBar';
    11 | import { useNodeStateStore } from '@/modules/nodeState';
    12 | 
    13 | export default function Home() {
    14 |   const selectedNodeId = useNodeStateStore((s) => s.selectedNodeId);
    15 |   const viewMode = useNodeStateStore((s) => s.viewMode);
    16 | 
    17 |   return (
    18 |     <PremiumLayout>
    19 |       <div className="flex flex-col h-full w-full overflow-hidden">
    20 |         {/* Top Toolbar */}
    21 |         <div className="h-12 border-b border-[#E5E5E5] flex items-center px-4 bg-white z-20 shrink-0">
    22 |           <TopToolbar />
    23 |         </div>
    24 | 
    25 |         {/* Main Area: Canvas + Right Panel OR Report View */}
    26 |         <div className="flex-1 flex overflow-hidden relative">
    27 |           {viewMode === 'workspace' && <FloatingControlBar />}
    28 |           {/* Always render canvas in background */}
    29 |           <div className="flex-1 relative overflow-hidden min-w-0">
    30 |             <SimulationWorkspace />
    31 |           </div>
    32 | 
    33 |           {/* Right Inspector Panel (Only Node Config in Workspace mode) */}

    35 |             <div className="w-[320px] shrink-0 border-l border-[#E5E5E5] bg-white overflow-y-auto custom-scrollbar relative z-10">
    36 |               <NodeConfigPanel />
    37 |             </div>
    38 |           )}
    39 | 
    40 |           {/* Report View Overlay */}
    41 |           {viewMode === 'report' && (
    42 |             <div className="absolute inset-0 z-20 flex">
    43 |               <AttackReportView />
    44 |             </div>
    45 |           )}
    46 |         </div>
    47 |       </div>
    48 |     </PremiumLayout>
    49 |   );
    50 | }

========================================================================================================================
────  TABLE 4 — RISK SCORING REFERENCE  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
========================================================================================================================


  STRUCTURAL RISK FORMULA
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  risk_score = exposure_weight × privilege_weight × asset_value_weight × depth_weight

  Component                 Formula                                            Range          
  ───────────────────────── ────────────────────────────────────────────────── ───────────────
  exposure_weight           1.0 + avg(exposure_score of path nodes)            1.1 – 2.0      
  privilege_weight          max(privilege_weight of path nodes)                0.8 – 1.5      
  asset_value_weight        max(asset_value of path nodes) / 5.0               0.2 – 2.0      
  depth_weight              min(1.0 + (path_length - 1) × 0.3, 2.5)            1.0 – 2.5      

  SEVERITY THRESHOLDS
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Score Range          Severity        Color          
  ──────────────────── ─────────────── ───────────────
  ≥ 13                 Critical        #8e44ad        
  9 – 12.99            High            #e74c3c        
  5 – 8.99             Medium          #f39c12        
  < 5                  Low             #2ecc71        

  BREACH TIME ESTIMATION
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Step Type                 Base Time (min)      Low Priv (×0.75)     High Priv (×1.4)    
  ───────────────────────── ──────────────────── ──────────────────── ────────────────────
  Initial Exploit           30                   22.5                 42                  
  Privilege Escalation      20                   15                   28                  
  Lateral Movement          15                   11.25                21                  
  Data Exfiltration         25                   18.75                35                  

  CONFIDENCE SCORING
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Base: 80
  Factor                                        Adjustment      Condition                               
  ───────────────────────────────────────────── ─────────────── ────────────────────────────────────────
  Direct exposure bonus                         +10             Public entry, ≤2 hops                   
  Known port vector bonus                       +5              Entry has risky ports                   
  Speculation penalty                           -8              No risky ports at entry                 
  Chain length penalty                          -5 per hop      Beyond 2 hops                           
  Lateral movement penalty                      -4 per step     Each lateral hop                        
  High exposure bonus                           +5              Entry exposure >= 0.8                   
  Low exposure penalty                          -5              Entry exposure < 0.3                    
  Privilege escalation penalty                  -3 per step     Each priv-esc step                      
  Exploit realism bonus                         +8              Path probability >= 0.7                 
  Exploit difficulty penalty                    -5              Path probability < 0.2                  
  Final: clamped to [10, 97]
